content.problemStatement:
On her way to ChefLand, Marichka noticed 10K10^K10K road signs (numbered 000 through 10Kâˆ’110^K - 110Kâˆ’1). For each valid iii, the sign with number iii had the integer iii written on one side and 10Kâˆ’iâˆ’110^K-i-110Kâˆ’iâˆ’1 written on the other side.

Now, Marichka is wondering â€” how many road signs have exactly two distinct decimal digits written on them (on both sides in total)? Since this number may be large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

For example, if K=3K = 3K=3, the two integers written on the road sign 363363363 are 363363363 and 636636636, and they contain two distinct digits 333 and 666, but on the road sign 362362362, there are integers 362362362 and 637637637, which contain four distinct digits â€” 222, 333, 666 and 777. On the road sign 111111, there are integers 111111 and 988988988, which contain three distinct digits â€” 111, 999 and 888.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer â€” the number of road signs with exactly two digits, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
